title: Review: Android foreground service + JMAP typing + identity restore
notes:
Maintainer risk: HIGH — Android lifecycle issues (wrong foregroundServiceType, no WakeLock, START_NOT_STICKY, SecurityException race) will generate user bug reports on first real-device test. The JMAP method_error bug will break any client that validates response method names. The controller dispose leaks are silent but will manifest as memory growth over time.
Confidence: 25% chance of missed bug. Uncertainty driven by: (1) the Android foreground service lifecycle has complex interaction with manufacturer-specific battery optimization that can't be audited statically; (2) the JMAP wire format was not compared against RFC 8620 exhaustively — only spot-checked; (3) MLS federation dispatch in relay.rs was reviewed for the authenticated-sender invariant but the key-package / group-state interactions were not traced end-to-end. Would raise confidence with: a real-device test on Android 13+ Pixel (stock) and a Samsung (aggressive battery saver), and a JMAP session trace against a reference server.
close_reason: all review findings resolved or deferred
